query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "measuring epistemic resilience of llms under misleading medical contex" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it measures the robustness and correctness of LLMs under misleading or incomplete medical contexts (via sentence dropout and open-ended self-diagnosis prompts), which directly aligns with the user's focus on "epistemic resilience" and "misleading medical context" in the medical domain.
